programmes.show_description: UFO Photos: As the world continues to evolve into the digital age, more and more people are capturing images in the sky that they are unable to explain. (S4, ep 11)
Mass Sightings: While many UFO sightings occur in rural locations with only a single eyewitness, there have been sightings over cities witnessed by hundreds. (S4, ep 16)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 6)
The Crop Circle Code: More than 10,000 crop circle formations have been documented and many display properties that defy explanation. Could they be of otherworldly origin? (S15, ep 2)
Casanova Gator: King of the Swamp Troy Landry gambles on Terral's secret honey hole, while Willie gives his son the reins for the first time and Ronnie baits with rotten duck. (S11, ep 10)
Snakes, Rattles and Rolls: Pickle faces her fear of big cats and impresses Troy with her natural hunting talent. Bill and Tes stumble upon a rumoured government testing facility. (S3, ep 2)
Short Staffed: With the shop short staffed, Seth struggles to stay on the ball. With each new high drama customer, he gets closer and closer to his breaking point. (S9, ep 2)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 26)
Welwyn Garden City 2: David Dickinson and his antique dealers travel to Welwyn Garden City, where they find an unusual letter opener and some flying helmets. (S10, ep 6)
Sheldon plays auctioneer when Scott decides to sell the entire collection of treasures in his man cave. (S4, ep 4)
Henry Cole, Allen Millyard and Guy Willison try to find vintage finds to sell in Kempton. (S3, ep 1)
We Fun-Did it!: Ivy drags Isaiah to Victorville as punishment for getting Cs on his report card, while Kenny wants to furnish his new house. (S15, ep 12)
San Brandi-Dino!: Brandi is fired up about expanding her brand in San Bernardino, while Rene is positive that everyone is out to get him. (S15, ep 13)
Redeem Girl Rumble: With employee relations not his strong suit, Seth tries to make amends by moving staff around. But his plan soon backfires. (S9, ep 5)
Busted: With their father/son relationship already on the rocks, a major miscommunication between Les and Seth threatens to land a key employee on the chopping block. (S9, ep 6)
Pawns of Anarchy: Rick gets his hands on a stellar coin but Corey flips out about the sizeable asking price. Meanwhile, Chum deals with an epic NWA Championship Wrestling belt.
The Cannibal: Jacob leads the Landry clan into Big Bull Bayou, while Chase hunts secluded channels with his tail boat. Daniel and Big Tee track a giant cannibal. (S11, ep 11)
The Night Serpent: Troy and Pickle attempt to track down a giant python known as the Night Serpent. Meanwhile, Tess convinces Bill to try out new hunting grounds. (S3, ep 3)
Sheldon plays auctioneer when Scott decides to sell the entire collection of treasures in his man cave. (S4, ep 4)
Your Pawpaw Loves You!: Lisa gets nostalgic as she returns to Moreno Valley, while Kenny welcomes a new granddaughter to the clan and searches for the perfect unit. (S15, ep 14)
Strings & Beans: Ivy takes on a supervisory role in Stanton which leads him to some Slavic success, while Brandi tries to help Danielle decorate her new apartment. (S15, ep 15)
The vintage restorers return for season four, challenging themselves to make money out of other people's automotive junk and classic collectables. (S4, ep 6)
Mystery of the Lost Civilisation: The discovery of the ancient Karahan Tepe megalithic site, displaying perplexing engineering skills, turns the archaeology world on its head. (S15, ep 3)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 8)
Money in the Bankshot: Chum chases a lead on a 1963 Fiat Microcar. Meanwhile, Rick and Corey make a wager on a set of billiard balls and Rick examines the holy grail of coins.
We Fun-Did it!: Ivy drags Isaiah to Victorville as punishment for getting Cs on his report card, while Kenny wants to furnish his new house. (S15, ep 12)
San Brandi-Dino!: Brandi is fired up about expanding her brand in San Bernardino, while Rene is positive that everyone is out to get him. (S15, ep 13)
The vintage restorers return for season four, challenging themselves to make money out of other people's automotive junk and classic collectables. (S4, ep 6)
Bigfoot and E.T.: Is the fabled cryptid a missing link in our own evolution or an intergalactic emissary of things to come? (S1, ep 1)
Yakutat: In this groundbreaking series, our episodes feature an array of opinions from a very select group of specialists. (S1, ep 5)
July 2022 marks the 75th anniversary of one of the most perplexing UFO mysteries: the alleged crash of an extra terrestrial craft at Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 7)

The Night Serpent: Troy and Pickle attempt to track down a giant python known as the Night Serpent. Meanwhile, Tess convinces Bill to try out new hunting grounds. (S3, ep 3)
Tipped Off: Customer drama is nothing new to the Golds, but when rumours emerge that an employee may be taking bribes, Ashley vows to get to the bottom of things. (S9, ep 4)
Join antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson as more collectors try to sell their items to dealers. Will they strike a deal or take a chance at auction instead? (S9, ep 27)
Welwyn Garden City 3: Interesting items include some ivory netsukes, a collection of Beatrix Potter figures and a pair of candlesticks that once belonged to actress Diana Dors. (S10, ep7)
A palm reader predicts that the guys will have a productive visit to Quebec, but one reluctant dealer could spoil their plans. (S4, ep 5)
The vintage restorers return for season four, challenging themselves to make money out of other people's automotive junk and classic collectables. (S4, ep 6)
Your Pawpaw Loves You!: Lisa gets nostalgic as she returns to Moreno Valley, while Kenny welcomes a new granddaughter to the clan and searches for the perfect unit. (S15, ep 14)
Strings & Beans: Ivy takes on a supervisory role in Stanton which leads him to some Slavic success, while Brandi tries to help Danielle decorate her new apartment. (S15, ep 15)
Watch Your Back: When Les allows a $60,000 watch to walk out the door on a handshake agreement, Seth and Ashley flip out. (S9, ep 7)
American Jewellery & Zoo: Les makes a deal to get the kids off his back, but when some interesting animals come through the door, it might be too much for him to resist. (S9, ep 8)
Rocket Man: Corey is offered the chance to buy an old dentist's chair, while an Andy Warhol Superman poster soars into the shop. Plus, Rick and Chum take a Cadillac for a spin.
Swamp on Fire: On the hottest day of the season, Daniel and Big Tee risk a trip into the scorching marsh lands. Plus, Ashley tries to fill her boat despite Ronnie's struggles. (S11, ep 12)
New Hunting Grounds: Troy and Pickle witness how far the pythons have spread as Zak and Aaron take on a new type of beast. Meanwhile, Bill and Tes tackle some uninvited guests. (S3, ep 4)
A palm reader predicts that the guys will have a productive visit to Quebec, but one reluctant dealer could spoil their plans. (S4, ep 5)
Pin Up or Shut Up: Dusty and Lupe pedal their way to an auction, but end up needing the truck. Meanwhile, Darrell pins his hopes on a small, overpriced locker. (S15, ep 16)
Rock, Paper, Winner: Back in Montebello, Gunter comes along to help Rene, while Darrell tries to make Kimber his queen. (S15, ep 17)
Henry, Allen and Guy dig through Matt XX's cast-offs, finding a vintage Chopper and a 1950s motorised bicycle. (S3, ep 2)
The Power of the Obelisks: Scholars identify Egypt's obelisks as monuments to the pharaohs, while ancient astronaut theorists suggest they may power communication with the gods. (S15, ep 4)

Welwyn Garden City 4: The team of antique dealers are in Welwyn Garden City today, where they sort the trash from the treasure, including a stunning bronze lizard by Bergman. (S10, ep 8)
Werewolves From Space: Bulletproof wolves attack a family farm, an April Fool's prank awakens a real-life horror and a driver picks up an unwanted and very hairy passenger. (S1, ep 2)
Tinley Park Lights: In this groundbreaking series, our episodes feature an array of opinions from a very select group of specialists. (S1, ep 8)
Our governments cannot hide anymore that alien contact is happening. Experts aim to answer the important question: where do we go from here?
